title: Displays, Cameras, Audio, and Accessible Output · Support Decision Defense
summary: A support panel compares near-miss cases for displays, cameras, audio, and accessible output and accepts only safe, authorized, causal, accessible, privacy-preserving, and independently defensible decisions. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for accessible audiovisual signal-path diagnosis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare LCD panel types, OLED, projectors, digitizers, backlights, inverters, touch layers, privacy filters, and failure signatures.
Relate physical size, pixel resolution, density, scaling, refresh, orientation, reflow, zoom, and readable workspace to the user task.
Trace source, GPU, port, cable, adapter, dock, sink, input selection, negotiation, power, and operating-system state before replacing parts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Provide keyboard, zoom, contrast, non-color, caption, transcript, screen-reader, haptic, and alternate-output routes with user verification.

hints: For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessible Output, name the user impact, fictional ticket, asset, environment, authorization, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, prevention, escalation, accessible documentation, and transfer boundary, Accessible Output precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessible Output, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A hybrid classroom has an intermittent external image, echo, camera privacy conflict, unreadable scaling, and color-only status. The quiz variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Provide keyboard, zoom, contrast, non-color, caption, transcript, screen-reader, haptic, and alternate-output routes with user verification.

For CompTIA A+ support Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Accessible Output, challenge the documented misconception with changed-user, device, version, hazard, baseline, symptom, timeline, access need, hypothesis, test result, repair, restart, recurrence, escalation, and controlled equipment-transfer evidence: Accessibility is complete when automated contrast checks pass.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.description: The decision applies Display Technologies to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Display Technologies.

checks.description: The decision applies Resolution Scaling to the changed stakeholder case.
checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Resolution Scaling.

checks.description: The evidence note explains the model, failure risk, and verification in the learner’s own words.
checks.failureMessage: Add a causal explanation using provide and keyboard plus specific case evidence.
